reforms.description: Germany has reformed its bankruptcy legislation to reduce the stay period for bankrupt entrepreneurs to facilitate a fresh start, thereby encouraging the reallocation of resources and fostering innovative startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform encourages entrepreneurship and innovation by allowing failed entrepreneurs to start anew, which can lead to increased economic activity and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly impacts the startup ecosystem by improving conditions for entrepreneurs, though it is not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Reform of bankruptcy legislation, most recently in 2012, reduced the stay period after which bankrupt entrepreneurs are cleared from remaining debt.

reforms.description: Germany is considering reforms to inheritance tax exemptions for family firms, which currently lock in capital and hinder the reallocation of resources, potentially improving productivity and intergenerational mobility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By easing tax burdens on family firms, the reform could enhance capital mobility and encourage investment in innovation, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently under consideration and has not yet been legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses a significant barrier for family businesses, which play a crucial role in the economy, but it is not a comprehensive overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government and parliament are considering reform of inheritance taxation.

reforms.description: The federal government plans to spend EUR 2.7 billion over three years to subsidize broadband deployment, aiming to ensure broadband speeds of 50 Mbit per second, and has introduced a special fund to support financially weak municipalities with EUR 3.5 billion for investment projects, including broadband infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in broadband infrastructure enhances connectivity, which is essential for innovation and economic development, particularly in rural areas.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The investment represents a significant commitment to improving infrastructure, though it is not a complete overhaul of the research infrastructure system.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government plans to spend EUR 2.7 billion (0.1% of annual GDP) over three years to subsidise broadband deployment in order to ensure broadband speeds of 50 Mbit per second.

reforms.description: Germany plans to implement a comprehensive e-procurement system to enhance the efficiency of public procurement, which is expected to promote innovation and investment in knowledge-based capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving procurement efficiency can lead to better allocation of resources and stimulate innovation in the public sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to modernize public procurement processes, which is significant but not a fundamental change to the system.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s plans to implement a comprehensive e-procurement system... are welcome.

reforms.description: The German government provides up to EUR 125 million for research and development projects focused on prevention and diet from 2013 to 2016, addressing major research gaps in public health.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Funding targeted at public health research can lead to innovations that improve health outcomes and reduce healthcare costs, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding programme has been operational since 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding addresses specific research needs in public health, which is significant but not a comprehensive reform of the R&D funding landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The government provides up to EUR 125 million for research-and-development projects in the period from 2013 to 2016.
